1. Florian Wirtz is worth every penny we paid! 
2. Ekitike is a bargain buy. 
3. Curtis Jones is so underrated but put in a commanding performance.
4. I hope fans who have been asking Slot to start Federico Chiesa will realise he is better used as a substitute.
5. Frimpong was under-used in the first half as we attacked mostly on the left but Slot wisely put Connor Bradley in and pushed Frimpong higher to right.
6. Bradley’s performance was immense and his late tackle prevented a certain goal. 
7. Defending set-pieces remains our weakest point. (Konate, I am sure your grandma can jump higher! 😂)
8. Alisson is one unsung hero, so consistent and dependable.
Come on boys, let’s push on and aim for the title ! YNWA

comment_content:
As a lifelong Liverpool supporter, I want to share how I’ve been viewing this season and everything that’s come with it. From my perspective, what we’re seeing is the natural growing pain of a team going through major transition. When you bring together a group of incredibly talented individuals—many of them new—and ask them to function as one unit, it’s never going to be seamless right away. Talent alone doesn’t guarantee chemistry.

I think it’s easy for us, as fans, to assume that because we had such a strong previous season, spent heavily in the transfer market, and added high-level players, everything would automatically click. But football rarely works that way. Losing someone like Diogo while integrating so many new pieces changes the entire rhythm of the team.

What I’ve appreciated most is watching Slot navigate all of this. You can see him constantly adjusting, experimenting, and searching for the right balance. What he may have envisioned at the start clearly didn’t unfold as planned, and instead of forcing it, he’s been willing to adapt. At times it’s looked chaotic, but to me it feels more like a manager playing chess—learning his pieces, testing combinations, and figuring out how everything truly fits together.

I can only imagine how disorienting it must have been early on—grinding out late wins, then suddenly hitting one of the toughest stretches this club has faced in decades. And yet, even through that, I’m starting to see the shape of something forming. Players are growing into their roles. Confidence is building. The cohesion is slowly emerging.

I even think Isak’s injury, as unfortunate as it is, could end up being a turning point for him. Sometimes stepping away—being forced to reset—can be exactly what a player needs. That time off might allow him to clear his head, reflect, and return with a refreshed mindset and renewed hunger. If he comes back more composed, more patient, and more in tune with the team around him, I truly believe he can become the dominant number nine we all know he can be.

Building a team is like building a family—you don’t find harmony overnight. But I genuinely believe we’re watching the foundation being laid for something special.
author: @TheFiyahMan_LFC
